name: Star of Texas Credit Union
description_short: Austin-based credit union serving Travis County members with bilingual support, ITIN lending, and CDFI designation. $10 minimum membership with no monthly checking fees.
description_long:
Star of Texas Credit Union is a mission-driven, non-profit financial institution based in Austin, Texas, currently in its 74th year of operation. The credit union is designated as a Community Development Financial Institution (CDFI), reflecting its commitment to serving economically disadvantaged individuals and underserved communities within Travis County and surrounding areas.

SOTCU offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including checking and savings accounts, auto loans, mortgages, credit cards with fixed rates, insurance products, and investment services. A distinguishing feature is their explicit focus on ITIN (Individual Taxpayer Identification Number) lending, allowing members without Social Security numbers to access loans, savings accounts, and mortgages. The credit union emphasizes accessibility through bilingual English-Spanish support, mobile banking via iOS and Android apps, and access to 5,000+ CO-OP shared branches nationwide.

What sets Star of Texas apart is their member-centric mission and community engagement. Membership requires only a $10 deposit in a savings account, with no monthly fees on checking accounts and no minimum balance requirements. Eligibility extends to anyone living, working, worshiping, or attending school in Travis County, plus Texas Workforce Commission employees and relatives of current members. In 2022, the credit union logged over 80 volunteer hours with community organizations like Caritas of Austin and Salvation Army. As of 2025, they've introduced an Annual Bonus Program crediting members based on average account balances across eligible accounts.

The main limitation is geographic eligibility restricted to Travis County and specific employee groups, making this institution unavailable to consumers outside its service area. Additionally, as a smaller credit union, product offerings and rates may not match larger national institutions. The website provides limited specific rate information, requiring prospective members to contact the credit union directly for detailed loan terms and savings rates.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: ITIN lending available—no Social Security number required for loans, savings, and mortgages
Minimal membership barrier—only $10 deposit to open savings account
No monthly fees on checking accounts and no minimum balance requirements
Bilingual support in English and Spanish for all members
CDFI-certified mission-driven institution focused on economically disadvantaged communities
Access to 5,000+ CO-OP shared branches nationwide for deposit and withdrawal
2025 Annual Bonus Program rewards members based on account balance history
Accounts NCUA-insured up to $250,000 by federal government organization
cons: Geographic eligibility limited to Travis County and specific employee groups—not available nationwide
Website provides limited specific rate information; must contact credit union directly for loan APRs and savings yields
As smaller credit union, product variety and competitive rates may lag national banks and larger credit unions
No mention of overdraft protection or fee waivers for low-income members
Limited information about mobile app features and functionality compared to larger institutions
best_for: Undocumented immigrants and ITIN holders seeking credit access and financial services
Travis County residents and employees seeking community-focused, non-profit banking with bilingual support
Members prioritizing mission-driven institutions that reinvest in local community development
Individuals with no minimum balance requirements seeking affordable, fee-free checking
diagnosis: Star of Texas Credit Union is best for Travis County residents, ITIN holders, and Spanish-speaking consumers seeking affordable, mission-driven banking with community-focused values and explicit support for undocumented immigrants. The main caveat is geographic and employment eligibility restrictions that make this institution unavailable outside its Travis County service area, and limited publicly available rate information requiring direct contact for loan and savings terms.
services: Checking accounts with no monthly fees and no minimum balance
ITIN lending for loans, mortgages, and credit products
Auto loans at low interest rates
Mortgage services including home purchase and refinance
CO-OP shared branch network access (5,000+ locations)
Bill pay and money transfer services
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
